title: A Throe Upon The Features
content:
A throe upon the features
    A hurry in the breath,
    An ecstasy of parting
    Denominated "Death," --

    An anguish at the mention,
    Which, when to patience grown,
    I 've known permission given
    To rejoin its own.
meaning:
"A Throe Upon The Features" is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
